Source   Edit  

Combat Effectiveness Rating (CER) System

Implements dice rolling and effectiveness calculation for EC4X combat resolution (Section 7.3.3)

Uses deterministic PRNG for reproducible tests

Deterministic PRNG (LCG - Linear Congruential Generator) Simple but sufficient for game dice rollsCER Calculation (Section 7.3.3)String formatting for loggingTesting helpers

Imports

types

Types

CombatRNG = object
  state*: uint64
Source   Edit  

Procs

proc `$`(cer: CERRoll): string {....raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Pretty print CER roll for logs Source   Edit   
proc applyDamage(damage: int; effectiveness: float): int {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Calculate actual damage: damage * CER Round up (Section 7.3.3) Source   Edit   
proc calculateHits(squadronAS: int; cerRoll: CERRoll): int {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Calculate total hits from squadron attack Total Hits = CER × Squadron_AS Source   Edit   
proc calculateModifiers(phase: CombatPhase; roundNumber: int; hasScouts: bool;
                        moraleModifier: int; isSurprise: bool; isAmbush: bool): int {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Calculate total CER modifiers for an attack Returns sum of all applicable modifiers Source   Edit   
proc deterministicRoll(seed: string; phase: CombatPhase; roundNum: int): CERRoll {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create deterministic CER roll for testing Uses same seed every time for reproducibility Source   Edit   
proc initRNG(seed: int64): CombatRNG {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initialize RNG with seed Seed format: gameId-turnNum-combatId-phaseNum-roundNum Source   Edit   
proc initRNG(seedString: string): CombatRNG {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initialize from string seed (for testing) Source   Edit   
proc next(rng: var CombatRNG): uint64 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Generate next random number Using LCG: Xn+1 = (a * Xn + c) mod m Source   Edit   
proc roll1d10(rng: var CombatRNG): int {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Roll a standard 1D10 die for CER Returns 1-10 (but we treat as 0-9 internally per spec) Spec uses "natural 9" for critical, which is roll result 9 Source   Edit   
proc rollCER(rng: var CombatRNG; phase: CombatPhase; roundNumber: int;
             hasScouts: bool; moraleModifier: int; isSurprise: bool = false;
             isAmbush: bool = false; desperationBonus: int = 0): CERRoll {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Roll for Combat Effectiveness Rating Returns CERRoll with all details

desperationBonus: Additional modifier when combat stalls (both sides fight desperately)

Source   Edit   
proc rollDie(rng: var CombatRNG; sides: int): int {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Roll a die with given number of sides (1-based) Returns value from 1 to sides (inclusive) Source   Edit   
proc simulateRolls(seed: int64; count: int): seq[int] {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Simulate multiple dice rolls to verify distribution Returns sequence of natural rolls (0-9) Source   Edit  

Exports

CERRoll, CERModifier
